title: Iran's 10-Point Plan and the Coerced Ceasefire
subtitle: A ceasefire that reduces immediate harm but rests on coercive legitimacy and unresolved enrichment risk.

sources.title: U.S. and Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire

judgmentMap.synthesis: The ceasefire reduces immediate harm but rests on coercive legitimacy and unresolved enrichment risk — a truce built on threats, not trust.

lensCards.label: Power
lensCards.sees: The entire sequence is disciplinary: escalate to threaten, then rebrand compliance as magnanimity. Whoever narrates this deal — who 'won,' who 'conceded' — controls the political reality.
lensCards.fears: Narrative capture. The ceasefire becomes proof that threats work, normalizing coercion as diplomacy.
lensCards.wouldDo: Watch the language, not the deal. Track who controls the frame in domestic media on both sides.

lensCards.label: Consequences
lensCards.sees: The Strait of Hormuz carries 20% of global oil. Every day of conflict cascades to every oil-importing economy, hitting the poorest hardest. A two-week pause produces massive immediate utility.
lensCards.fears: Accepting enrichment without safeguards creates proliferation risk whose long-tail cost dwarfs short-term gains.
lensCards.wouldDo: Take the deal as a starting framework. The costs of resumed bombing far exceed imperfect terms — but demand hard safeguards on enrichment.

lensCards.label: Hidden motives
lensCards.sees: Both sides perform strength from vulnerability. Iran's 10 points are not just security positions — they are demands for dignity after humiliation. The 'full withdrawal' demand is a symbolic reversal of decades of dominance.
lensCards.fears: A deal built on one side's humiliation will not hold. Ressentiment drives several of Iran's points more than strategy does.
lensCards.wouldDo: Test whether both sides can claim victory. Mutual face-saving may hold. Mutual humiliation guarantees collapse.

lensCards.label: Principles
lensCards.sees: Threatening civilizational annihilation to extract compliance is categorically impermissible regardless of outcome. Using millions of lives as a bargaining chip violates the principle that persons may never be treated merely as means.
lensCards.fears: Peace built on existential terror poisons the legitimacy of whatever agreement follows. The method contaminates the result.
lensCards.wouldDo: Insist on the UN resolution demand — it is the mechanism most likely to transform a coerced truce into legitimate, universalizable peace.

actorMap.name: United States
actorMap.stake: Claim victory, prevent enrichment, maintain regional control
actorMap.exposure: Global backlash, energy market disruption, legitimacy deficit

actorMap.name: Iran
actorMap.leverage: Strait of Hormuz control, enrichment capability
actorMap.stake: Sovereignty recognition, sanction relief, dignity restoration
actorMap.exposure: Continued bombardment, economic collapse, regime instability

actorMap.name: Israel
actorMap.stake: Prevent Iranian nuclear capability at any cost
actorMap.exposure: Position largely absent from reporting — a major unknown

actorMap.name: Global oil markets
actorMap.stake: Strait of Hormuz reopening, price stability
actorMap.exposure: Prolonged disruption hits developing nations hardest

scenarioCards.name: Fragile truce
scenarioCards.whatHappens: Shipping resumes, rhetoric cools, enrichment unresolved. The two-week window expires without a framework, leading to rolling extensions with no permanent settlement.
scenarioCards.trigger: Both sides prefer ambiguity to collapse
scenarioCards.watchFor: Extension announcements without new terms on enrichment

scenarioCards.name: Coerced deal
scenarioCards.whatHappens: Ceasefire holds briefly but legitimacy collapses. Iran accepts terms under duress, domestic opposition mounts, hardliners gain ground, conflict returns within months.
scenarioCards.trigger: Iran signs under economic desperation without face-saving provisions
scenarioCards.watchFor: Iranian domestic protests against the deal, hardliner rhetoric escalating

scenarioCards.name: Structured de-escalation
scenarioCards.whatHappens: UN-backed framework emerges. Partial sanction relief, monitored enrichment safeguards, Hormuz passage protocols formalized. Slow, imperfect, but durable.
scenarioCards.trigger: EU or UN inserts itself as guarantor, both sides accept external oversight
scenarioCards.watchFor: UN Security Council draft resolution, EU diplomatic mission to Tehran

hiddenAssumptions: That a two-week ceasefire is a step toward peace rather than a pause before escalation
That Pakistan has the diplomatic weight to mediate between the US and Iran
That Iran's 10 points are a genuine negotiating position rather than a domestic performance
That the US threat of civilizational annihilation was a negotiating tactic rather than a policy position
judgmentMap.whatThisMisses: Classified military realities, back-channel agreements, Iran's clerical-military internal politics, and Israel's position on the plan — which may matter more than any lens can capture from public reporting.
